overview: From 2026-05-21 to 2026-05-22, elizaos/eliza had 13 new PRs (30 merged), 1 new issues, and 12 active contributors.

topIssues.title: Agent Identity Verification dApp — built on your ERC-8004 contracts
topIssues.author: AtlasNexusTech
topIssues.body:
Hey ElizaOS team,

I built a verification dApp that runs on the same ERC-8004 contracts your plugin-wallet uses (0x8004A169... on Base/Celo/Linea/Scroll/Avalanche).

→ **https://atlasnexus.tech/verify/**

**What it does:**
- Register agent identity (mint ERC-8004 NFT)
- Lookup any address on-chain across supported chains
- Sign/verify messages with wallet (cryptographic identity proof)
- Live on-chain stats
- 100% client-side, zero backend

**Why this matters for Eliza:**
Your ERC-8004 plugin handles the SDK side. My dApp provides the user-facing verification interface — a way for Eliza agent operators to visually register, verify, and manage their agent's on-chain identity.

This could be integrated as:
1. A standalone verification tool for Eliza agents
2. An embeddable widget in the Eliza dashboard
3. A reference implementation for the ERC-8004 ecosystem

Would love to discuss integration. I'm also offering this as infrastructure to AI agent companies.

Alexandre
https://atlasnexus.tech/verify/

topPRs.title: Shaw/wip snapshot 2026 05 21
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
# Relates to # Risks # Background ## What does this PR do? ## What kind of change is this? # Documentation changes needed? # Testing ## Where should a reviewer start? ## Detailed testing steps

Greptile Summary

Review completed and submitted via submit_review tool.

Confidence Score: 3/5

The Tails persistence refactor and Phantom SIWE fix are well-structured, but the duplicate smartglasses view ID and missing plugin-google stub could cause silent runtime failures. The hearwear plugin registers three views sharing id: smartglasses with no confirmation that consumers key on (id, viewType). The prepare-milady-app-overlay.mjs asymmetry leaves plugin-google without a stub unlike the other removed packages. plugins/plugin-hearwear/src/index.ts (duplicate view IDs) and packages/os/linux/variants/milady-tails/scripts/prepare-milady-app-overlay.mjs (missing plugin-google stub) Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["wip: pending chip and training script up..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/90da063ee653f92f89d02a248ac9f94abf524509) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33186697)

topPRs.title: fix(chip): keep source-only riscv checks clean
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

- add a `--report` override to `scripts/check_linux_external_bsp.py` so callers can write host-local status under `build/reports`
- route the minimum-Linux nested external-BSP check to `build/reports/linux-external-bsp-status.json` instead of dirtying tracked release evidence under `docs/evidence/...`
- make optional Hypothesis/CAD/rendering chip tests skip and typecheck cleanly whether optional Python deps are absent locally or present in the Docker image
- add regression coverage proving custom external-BSP reports do not mutate tracked evidence
- normalize existing `packages/chip` AI-EDA/TEE script ruff/format/mypy debt so the Docker regression gate can run the chip lint/typecheck target cleanly
- keep the E1 IOMMU/cache/BPU RTL clean under the `eliza-soc-tools` Verilator gates by removing inferred combinational latches and Verilator-unsupported reset/allocation loop patterns
- include `e1_soc_integrated_tb_test_cross_domain_interfaces` in the release cocotb evidence manifest and make `evidence-regression-test` produce the synthesis/formal/verilator/cocotb artifacts that `pipeline_check.py` requires
- keep the prototype status dashboard checker stable when CI has generated transient synthesis/cocotb/verilator/formal/qemu artifacts but the tracked dashboard intentionally remains a conservative source-tree baseline
- merge latest `origin/develop` (`7bddb5d46a`) into this branch and typecheck the newly merged AI-EDA helper scripts so the branch is current with Shaw's latest RISC-V/chip work
- remove a duplicate `pluginWorkerRuntimeSrc` declaration in `packages/app-core/vitest.config.ts` from the merged develop tree so Release Workflow Contract can parse the app-core Vitest config

## Scope

RISC-V / E1 chip source-only and Docker-gate validation hygiene. This does not touch USB/Tails work, does not claim board/silicon evidence, and does not promote the Debian riscv64 image.

The broad script-format commit is intentionally mechanical and separate from the functional report-routing fix: CI reached `docker-regression` and failed on pre-existing chip-wide ruff/format debt, so this PR includes the minimum chip script normalization needed for that gate.

The RTL commits are gate-driven: after lint/typecheck passed in Docker, the checked Verilator targets exposed real E1 IOMMU/cache/BPU RTL issues. The fixes keep behavior equivalent while making the source acceptable to the checked Verilator version and the cross-domain cocotb integration target.

The dashboard commit is similarly gate-driven: `ci-fast` and evidence-regression runs create `build/**` evidence before `prototype-status-dashboard-check`, while the tracked dashboard documents the conservative source-tree baseline. The checker now tolerates that volatile generated-artifact drift only for known generated-output subsystems and still rejects stale nonvolatile dashboard rows.

The app-core Vitest change is a minimal CI-unblocker from the merged latest develop tree: Release Workflow Contract failed before tests could run because the config declared the same const twice.

## Validation

Local/source checks:

- `PATH=/tmp/eliza-riscv-ruff-venv/bin:$PATH make -C packages/chip lint` -> ruff lint/format, JSON syntax, and git whitespace passed; local shellcheck/yamllint reported blocked because those tools are not installed locally
- `PATH=/tmp/eliza-riscv-ruff-venv/bin:$PATH make -C packages/chip typecheck` -> mypy, Python syntax, platform contract schema, project plan schema, and software BSP schema passed; external BSP promotion evidence remains correctly reported as `BLOCKED`
- same `make -C packages/chip typecheck` rerun after installing `hypothesis` and `cadquery` into the temp venv to match the Docker optional-dependency branch
- `python3 -m compileall -q packages/chip/scripts packages/chip/benchmarks/parsers packages/chip/compiler/runtime packages/os/linux/variants/elizaos-debian-riscv64/scripts`
- `python3 -m unittest discover -s packages/chip/scripts -p 'test_*.py'` -> 182 tests, OK, 5 skipped
- `python3 -m unittest discover -s packages/chip/benchmarks/parsers/tests -p 'test_*.py'` -> 16 tests, OK, 1 skipped
- `python3 -m unittest discover -s packages/os/linux/variants/elizaos-debian-riscv64/scripts -p 'test_*.py'` -> 37 tests, OK, 16 skipped
- focused Chipyard/Renode/QEMU status tests passed:
  - `python3 scripts/test_chipyard_verilator_preflight.py`
  - `python3 scripts/test_chipyard_verilator_linux_smoke.py`
  - `python3 scripts/test_chipyard_verilator_smoke_robustness.py`
  - `python3 scripts/test_renode_status.py`
  - `python3 scripts/test_qemu_smoke_status.py`
- dashboard validation:
  - `python3 packages/chip/scripts/test_check_prototype_status_dashboard.py`
  - `python3 packages/chip/scripts/check_prototype_status_dashboard.py`
- cross-domain manifest regression:
  - `python3 packages/chip/scripts/test_cocotb_pipeline_targets.py`
  - `python3 -m compileall -q packages/chip/scripts/check_cocotb_results.py packages/chip/scripts/test_cocotb_pipeline_targets.py`

Docker validation from `packages/chip`:

- `docker build -t eliza-soc-tools .`
- `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work eliza-soc-tools make rtl-check`
- `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work eliza-soc-tools make lint typecheck`
- `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work eliza-soc-tools python3 scripts/test_check_prototype_status_dashboard.py`
- `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work eliza-soc-tools python3 scripts/check_prototype_status_dashboard.py`
- `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work eliza-soc-tools make lint typecheck prototype-status-dashboard-check`
- after merging latest `origin/develop`: `docker run --rm -u "$(id -u):$(id -g)" -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work/packages/chip eliza-soc-tools make evidence-regression-test`
  - synthesis, Verilator smoke, NPU GEMM smoke, Yosys formal fallback manifest, cocotb chip/NPU/tiny CPU, and cross-domain integration cocotb all passed
  - `e1_soc_integrated_tb_test_cross_domain_interfaces`: 11 tests, 11 passed
  - strict release gate regression passed
  - final line: `evidence regression tests complete`
- after merging latest `origin/develop`: `docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/work" -w /work/packages/chip eliza-soc-tools make lint typecheck` passed
- app-core Vitest config fix:
  - `git diff --check` passed
  - `rg -n "const pluginWorkerRuntimeSrc|plugin-worker-runtime" packages/app-core/vitest.config.ts` shows one declaration and the existing aliases
  - full local Release Workflow Contract was not run because this sparse RISC-V worktree does not have the app-core dependency tree installed; the CI failure was a parse-time duplicate declaration and this patch removes that duplicate directly
- final hygiene:
  - `git fetch --prune origin` showed `origin/develop` still at merged commit `7bddb5d46a`
  - `git diff --check` passed
  - `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard packages/chip` produced no untracked nonignored chip files

## Remaining RISC-V Work

The current honest RISC-V state is still blocked on real promotion evidence: qemu/grub/agent-live evidence for the Debian rv64 image, generated-AP Linux boot beyond banner-only, E1 platform contract vs Chipyard ABI reconciliation, and target-side `/dev/e1-npu` smoke transcripts. This PR removes local source-check friction, prevents tracked evidence churn, and gets the chip Docker lint/typecheck/RTL/cocotb/dashboard/evidence-regression gates clean while those larger proofs are collected.

topPRs.title: fix: stabilize gpt-oss routing and opencode handoffs
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary This PR stabilizes the gpt-oss/OpenCode live path by tightening the generic planner/tool contracts rather than adding deployment-specific prompt hacks. It includes: - Stabilized `TASKS` / OpenCode app handoff behavior so completed sub-agent app builds return clean final URLs instead of visible thread/progress spam, stale attachment leakage, duplicate finals, or session/cancel noise. - Preserved Discord connector reply references so final bot messages stay attached to the triggering prompt. - Added malformed Stage 1 tool-call recovery for model outputs that contain valid tool calls in less-than-ideal response shapes. - Throttled and deduped autonomy/batch-drain task loops so repeated persisted drain tasks do not flood trajectories. - Preserved a single successful tool's explicit `userFacingText` over evaluator paraphrase, preventing exact paths/numbers from being hallucinated during final synthesis. ## Why Live gpt-oss-120b + OpenCode testing exposed several real issues in Discord trajectories: - App builds could create visible task-thread / emoji progress noise even though the sub-agent finished successfully. - Planner/action routing could recover from bad model shapes but needed a generic retry path. - Autonomy drain tasks could duplicate and produce noisy repeated trajectories. - Final response synthesis could paraphrase exact tool output and mutate factual strings, e.g. `/home/milady/.bun` became `/home/milody/.bun` even though the shell tool returned the correct verified `userFacingText`. ## Validation Focused code validation on latest `origin/develop`: - `packages/core`: focused suite `164/164`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun run build` - `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator`: focused suite `150/150`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun run build` - `plugins/plugin-coding-tools`: `bun test --timeout 10000 src/actions/bash.test.ts` -> `38/38`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun run build` - `plugins/plugin-sql`: `bun test src/__tests__/integration/task.real.test.ts` -> `5/5`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun run build` - `git diff --check` and `git diff --cached --check` clean Live Discord/API/log/trajectory validation after restart from local source: - Disk/RAM prompt `1506974790538362920` -> reply `1506974985942601839`, trajectory `tj-310e968b11fa94` - `SHELL`, 1 tool call, 0 tool failures, 0 evaluator failures - no Discord thread, no reactions/progress markers - verified final path stayed `/home/milady/.bun`; no `/home/milody` typo - BTC prompt `1506973638878957578` -> reply `1506973663180886127`, trajectory `tj-2cdd689447708e` - `SHELL`, 1 tool call, 0 tool failures, 0 evaluator failures - App-build prompt `1506973704100380755` -> reply `1506973786526711839`, trajectories `tj-2d1a1d8b231a54` and `tj-2d5c9fe64d849e` - `TASKS` / OpenCode path, 0 tool failures - no visible Discord thread, no reactions/progress markers - final URL: https://nubilio.org/apps/micro-journal-prompt/ (HTTP 200 verified) Evidence JSON is saved locally under: - `/home/milady/session-summary/live-clean-opencode-20260521-final/` - `/home/milady/session-summary/live-clean-opencode-20260521-preserve/` ## Notes This intentionally does not include the rejected regex-based safety detour from local testing. That approach overblocked unrelated disk/app prompts and was removed. Any regulated-commerce policy work should be handled separately through the normal safety/policy layer, not hard-coded routing regexes in this PR.

Greptile Summary

This PR stabilizes the gpt-oss/OpenCode live path by tightening several planner and routing contracts: it adds malformed Stage 1 tool-call recovery, throttles/deduplicates autonomy drain tasks, preserves a single tool's exact `userFacingText` over evaluator paraphrase, adds configurable sub-agent progress policy (compact/threaded/silent with a startup delay), and restores Discord reply references for TASKS-spawned bot messages. - **Core planner changes** (`planner-loop.ts`, `action-tiering.ts`, `message.ts`): `preferredFinalMessageFromToolOrModel` now prefers a single successful tool's `userFacingText` over the evaluator's synthesized reply; Stage 1 retry logic is extended to catch malformed (present-but-empty) `HANDLE_RESPONSE` tool calls; canonical action names are registered before similes to prevent simile-based capture of real action surfaces. - **Orchestrator + Discord** (`plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/index.ts`, `plugin-discord/service.ts`): Sub-agent progress delivery is gated by a configurable delay and mode policy, clearing timers on `task_complete`/`task_error`; Discord reply references are now propagated through `originConnectorMessageId` so final bot messages reply to the triggering prompt; `sub-agent-router.ts` adds verify-retry event suppression and makes `requireFresh` opt-in so files pre-dating a session start are no longer treated as dead URLs. - **Infrastructure** (`task-drain.ts`, `batcher.ts`, `dispatcher.ts`, `plugin-sql/base.ts`): Duplicate persisted drain tasks are deleted on startup; recurring batcher sections are throttled by `minCycleMs`; `agentId` is added to `getTasksByName`/`getTask` return shapes needed by `matchesTask`.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge with minor cleanup; all changes are well-tested and address real live-trajectory issues observed in production. The three flagged issues are all non-blocking: a dead `chunks.length === 1` condition in the Discord multi-chunk path (doesn't affect the final reply behavior), a `shouldRun` predicate evaluated after the recurring-section timestamp is already advanced (potential over-suppression in niche section configs), and a redundant `typeof serialized === "string"` guard in `isUnsupportedCancelError`. None introduce incorrect behavior in the primary live paths. The core behavioral changes — Stage 1 retry extension, tool-text priority, action-tiering canonical-first registration, delay-based progress gating, and verify-retry suppression — are all clearly intentional, consistently tested, and address concrete trajectory failures observed in live runs. `plugins/plugin-discord/service.ts` for the dead condition in the multi-chunk send path; `packages/core/src/utils/prompt-batcher/batcher.ts` for the mark-before-shouldRun ordering.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/core/src/runtime/planner-loop.ts | Adds `preferredFinalMessageFromToolOrModel` that gives a single successful tool's `userFacingText` priority over the evaluator's paraphrase to prevent hallucination of exact paths/values; logic is correct and well-covered by the new happy-path test. | | packages/core/src/services/message.ts | Extends the Stage 1 empty-completion retry to also detect and retry malformed `HANDLE_RESPONSE` tool calls (tool present but arguments unparseable); retry limit and logging are correctly updated, and the new test covers the recovery path. | | packages/core/src/features/autonomy/service.ts | Restricts broad participant-room scanning to an explicit opt-in flag (`AUTONOMY_INCLUDE_ALL_ROOMS=true`), preventing inadvertent room enumeration; hoists autonomy memory fetch before the room-ID check so thought context is always included. | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/index.ts | Large addition of configurable progress policy (`compact`/`threaded`/`silent`) and a timer-based delay mechanism that suppresses early sub-agent progress noise; delay logic is largely correct but a narrow race where the timer fires just as `task_complete` is processed can emit a stale progress message after the completion one. | | plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/sub-agent-router.ts | Adds session-event suppression after a verify-retry handoff and filters internal `session/cancel` MCP errors; changes `requireFresh` from opt-out (`!== false`) to opt-in (`=== true`), intentionally allowing pre-session-start files to pass verification, which the new stale-mtime test validates. | | plugins/plugin-discord/service.ts | Adds Discord reply-reference propagation via `discordReplyReferenceFromContent`; single-chunk path is correct and tested, but the multi-chunk path contains a dead `chunks.length === 1` condition that can never be true inside `if (chunks.length > 1)`. | | packages/core/src/utils/batch-queue/task-drain.ts | Deduplicates stale persisted drain tasks on startup by deleting all but the first matching task; uses `Promise.allSettled` so a single delete failure won't abort the rest. | | packages/core/src/utils/prompt-batcher/batcher.ts | Adds `minCycleMs` throttle for recurring sections via `_markRecurringSectionAttempt`; timestamp is marked before `shouldRun` is evaluated, which could cause over-suppression when `shouldRun` gates a section that becomes ready within the cycle window. | | plugins/plugin-sql/src/base.ts | Adds the missing `agentId` field to `getTasksByName` and `getTask` return mappings, needed by `TaskDrain.matchesTask` to filter tasks by agent; simple and targeted fix. | | packages/core/src/utils/prompt-batcher/dispatcher.ts | Passes the maximum `maxRetries` across a call-plan's sections into the model invocation options; straightforward and well-tested by the new dispatcher test. | | packages/core/src/runtime/action-tiering.ts | Refactors candidate matching to build a canonical-owners map so real action names take priority over simile-based matches, preventing a simile of `TASKS` from capturing the `TASKS` action surface. | | packages/core/src/features/basic-capabilities/providers/attachments.ts | Skips attachment prompt rendering for `sub_agent` source messages, preventing stale sub-agent progress attachments from leaking into subsequent prompt context. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant U as User (Discord) participant DS as DiscordService participant MH as MessageHandler (Stage1) participant PL as PlannerLoop participant OR as OrchestratorPlugin participant SA as SubAgent (OpenCode) participant SR as SubAgentRouter U->>DS: Send prompt message DS->>MH: runV5MessageRuntimeStage1() MH-->>MH: Stage1 retry if empty/malformed tool call MH->>PL: planned_reply (TASKS action) PL->>OR: TASKS action handler OR->>SA: spawn sub-agent session OR-->>DS: (no progress yet, within delayMs) SA-->>OR: progress events OR-->>DS: emitProgress after delayMs (compact text) SA-->>SR: task_complete event SR->>SR: verifyMappedLocalUrl (requireFresh opt-in) SR-->>OR: clearDelayedProgress(sessionId) Note over PL: Single tool userFacingText wins over evaluator paraphrase PL->>DS: finalMessage with originConnectorMessageId DS->>U: Reply referencing original prompt message ```

Comments Outside Diff (1)

 1. `packages/core/src/utils/prompt-batcher/batcher.ts`, line 698-710 ([link](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/blob/4ba5130529b67ab5c621281f6aa397fe106759f4/packages/core/src/utils/prompt-batcher/batcher.ts#L698-L710)) `_markRecurringSectionAttempt` is called before the `shouldRun` predicate is evaluated. If `shouldRun` returns `false` (e.g., "no pending work yet"), the timestamp is still advanced, so the section will be suppressed for the full `minCycleMs` window even though it never produced any model output. For sections that combine `shouldRun` with a long `minCycleMs`, this can delay the first actual execution if the predicate flips to `true` shortly after a skipped cycle.
Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(core): preserve single-tool user-fac..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/4ba5130529b67ab5c621281f6aa397fe106759f4) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33219863) > Greptile also left **2 inline comments** on this PR.

topPRs.title: feat(plugin): remote-mode runtime + bridge + isolated-process worker (P1)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
P1 of the **Plugin/mode unification**, stacked on PR #7844. Where P0 added the vocabulary, manifest schema, and wire-envelope types, **P1 makes remote-mode plugins actually run**: a worker bootstrap, a host-side bridge that mirrors worker surfaces into the agent runtime, and an isolated-process worker runner so plugins requesting `isolation: \"isolated-process\"` no longer silently downgrade. ## Commits | Commit | Scope | |---|---| | `feat(plugin-worker-runtime): scaffold worker bootstrap for remote-mode plugins` | New package `@elizaos/plugin-worker-runtime`. Worker-side bootstrap: imports the author's Plugin object, walks surfaces, announces, dispatches inbound `worker-rpc`. Modules: `envelope` (WorkerChannel + Worker postMessage adapter), `error` (Error round-trip with boundary marker), `runtime-proxy` (RuntimeProxy + buildRuntimeProxyApi for the subset of IAgentRuntime remote plugins call), `descriptor` (Plugin → JSON announce), `dispatch` (`worker-rpc` → live handler), `bootstrap` (entrypoint). | | `feat(agent): RemotePluginBridge — host-side wiring for remote-mode plugins` | New `packages/agent/src/services/remote-plugin-bridge.ts`. On `worker-announce-plugin`, materialises a stub `Plugin` with handlers that proxy to the worker via `worker-rpc` and registers it via `runtime.registerPlugin(...)`. Inbound `host-rpc` from the worker is dispatched against the real `IAgentRuntime` (getService / useModel / getMemory / createMemory / updateMemory / emitEvent / getSetting / setSetting / composeState). | | `feat(electrobun): IsolatedProcessWorkerRunner — Bun.spawn for isolation:isolated-process` | `SubprocessWorkerHandle` (newline-delimited JSON over stdin/stdout, stderr inherits to host log, SIGTERM-then-SIGKILL termination with 2s grace). `IsolatedProcessWorkerRunner` + `AdaptiveWorkerRunner` (picks per-plugin between Bun-Worker and Bun-subprocess based on `permissionsGranted.isolation`). Default `RemotePluginHost` now uses the adaptive runner; the warn-and-fallback path is deleted. Worker side gets `createSubprocessChannel()` + `createDefaultChannel()` (env-sniffs `ELIZA_REMOTE_PLUGIN_CHANNEL=stdio`). | ## Surfaces wired in P1 | Surface | Worker side (dispatch.ts) | Host side (RemotePluginBridge) | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | `provider` | ✅ | ✅ | Pure JSON in / out | | `event` | ✅ | ✅ (fires on worker via `worker-rpc`, listens via `host-rpc emitEvent`) | Pub/sub both directions | | `model` | ✅ | ✅ | JSON in / out; streaming via `stream-chunk`/`stream-end` lands in P2 | | `action` | ✅ (callback no-op) | ✅ (callback param dropped) | Action `validate` round-trips via `worker-rpc` too. Full callback marshalling is P1.5 | | `evaluator` | ✅ | n/a in P1 bridge (TBD next iteration) | Worker dispatcher handles inbound invocations | | `route` | ✅ (path through) | deferred to P2 | Routes need agent HTTP server registration + SSE/multipart handling | | `service` | rejected with typed error | n/a | P2 with explicit `static rpcMethods` opt-in | | `views`/`widgets`/`componentTypes` | passed through as JSON | passed through to existing views registry | The agent's `/api/views/:id/bundle.js` already supports `bundleUrl` for remote plugins, so no new code needed | ## Isolation - `isolation: \"shared-worker\"` (default) → Bun `Worker`. Cheap, shared crash domain. - `isolation: \"isolated-process\"` → `Bun.spawn`. Crash domain isolated. Required for sub-agent plugins (Claude Code, Codex, opencode runners) and any plugin with `bun.process: true`. The wire envelope is identical in both cases — switching is a channel-construction choice, not a protocol fork. ## What's still TODO - **Action callbacks** (P1.5): worker-side `CallbackProxy` whose invocations round-trip to the host's real `callback` via `worker-rpc surface=\"action.callback\"`. Most action handlers either don't use callback or rely on the orchestrator's progress channel today. - **Service surface** (P2): explicit `static rpcMethods: string[]` opt-in, then `ServiceProxy` synthesis. - **Route surface** (P2): agent HTTP server registers stubs; SSE + multipart support; 25MB body cap for remote routes. - **View-host shim packages** (P2): `@elizaos/plugin-host-shim-{electrobun,ios,android,web}` — ~50 LOC each, all conform to the `PluginHostShim` contract. - **`runtime.installRemotePlugin(...)`** (P3): agent-generated plugin pipeline with inline source delivery + permission narrowing. ## Verify Skipped on the worktree (no `bun install`); CI on this PR will validate. The two key invariants are: 1. A `Plugin` with `mode: \"remote\"` reaches the runtime as a normal-shaped Plugin object via the bridge. 2. A subprocess-based worker can complete the announce → dispatch → result round-trip over stdio with the same envelope the Worker channel uses. ## Base branch Stacked on `shaw/p0-plugin-mode-unify` (PR #7844). Merge order: P0 first, then P1 rebases automatically. If P0 is reshaped, P1 needs a rebase pass — most P1 code is new files so conflicts will be in `agent/services/index.ts` and `agent/package.json` only. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Greptile Summary

This PR introduces P1 of the plugin/mode unification: a new `@elizaos/plugin-worker-runtime` package (worker-side bootstrap, descriptor builder, RPC dispatcher, transport adapters, error serialization), a host-side `RemotePluginBridge` that materialises stub plugins from worker announce descriptors, and an `IsolatedProcessWorkerRunner`/`AdaptiveWorkerRunner` pair that honours `isolation: "isolated-process"` via `Bun.spawn` instead of silently falling back. - **New package `@elizaos/plugin-worker-runtime`**: Worker bootstrap walks plugin surfaces, registers handlers in a local registry, announces descriptors to the host, and enters a steady-state RPC dispatch loop; transport is abstracted over a `WorkerChannel` interface with implementations for both Bun Worker postMessage and subprocess stdio. - **`RemotePluginBridge` (host side)**: On `worker-announce-plugin` it synthesises action/provider/event/model stubs whose handlers proxy back via `worker-rpc`, and dispatches inbound `host-rpc` calls against the real `IAgentRuntime` — including an unrestricted `setSetting` that warrants a permission gate. - **`IsolatedProcessWorkerRunner`**: Spawns a fresh `bun` subprocess per isolated-process plugin using newline-delimited JSON over stdio; the `AdaptiveWorkerRunner` default picks between Bun Worker and subprocess based on the plugin manifest's `isolation` field, but currently forwards all host environment variables to the subprocess.

Confidence Score: 3/5

The core announce-before-init ordering bug means the host can invoke worker handlers against an uninitialised plugin, and the process.env spread and unrestricted setSetting create security exposure that is easy to overlook since isolated-process mode is specifically designed for higher-risk code. Three distinct defects span the two most security-sensitive paths in the PR: all host environment variables are forwarded to subprocess workers, defeating the confidentiality goal of process isolation; remote workers can overwrite arbitrary host-runtime settings without a permission check; and the worker announces before its init completes, opening a window where dispatched handlers run against uninitialised state. packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/native/remote-plugin-host.ts (process.env spread + EOF buffer drain), packages/agent/src/services/remote-plugin-bridge.ts (setSetting gap + duplicate announce), packages/plugin-worker-runtime/src/bootstrap.ts (announce-before-init ordering)

Security Review

 - **`IsolatedProcessWorkerRunner` (`remote-plugin-host.ts` line 297)**: Spreads `...process.env` into every isolated-process subprocess. All host secrets (API keys, database credentials, tokens) are forwarded to what is explicitly intended to be an untrusted/sandboxed process. - **`setSetting` host-rpc handler (`remote-plugin-bridge.ts` lines 464–468)**: Any remote worker can call `runtime.setSetting(key, value)` with an arbitrary key and value. No permission check is enforced, allowing a compromised plugin to overwrite API keys, model endpoints, or other sensitive configuration values.

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/plugin-worker-runtime/src/bootstrap.ts | Worker entrypoint — sends worker-announce-plugin before plugin.init completes, creating a race where the host can dispatch worker-rpc calls into an uninitialized plugin. | | packages/agent/src/services/remote-plugin-bridge.ts | Host-side bridge — setSetting host-rpc allows any remote worker to overwrite arbitrary runtime settings; no duplicate-announce guard can orphan plugin registrations. | | packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/src/native/remote-plugin-host.ts | IsolatedProcessWorkerRunner spreads all host process.env secrets into subprocess workers; SubprocessWorkerHandle drops unterminated final line on EOF. | | packages/plugin-worker-runtime/src/dispatch.ts | Worker-side RPC dispatcher — clean surface-routed dispatch with explicit noop-callback stub and exhaustiveness check; service/tests surfaces correctly rejected. | | packages/plugin-worker-runtime/src/envelope.ts | Transport adapters for Worker postMessage and stdio newline-delimited JSON — well-structured with clean close/unsubscribe lifecycle; env-sniff channel selection is correct. | | packages/plugin-worker-runtime/src/runtime-proxy.ts | RuntimeProxy and buildRuntimeProxyApi — registerEvent listed in SUPPORTED_RUNTIME_METHODS but throws immediately; otherwise clean pending-map timeout handling. | | packages/plugin-worker-runtime/src/descriptor.ts | Plugin-to-JSON descriptor builder with stable rpc-id allocation and handler registry — straightforward walk of all surfaces with correct JSON substitution. | | packages/plugin-worker-runtime/src/error.ts | Error round-trip serialization — correctly handles Error/primitive/object causes with clear boundary-frame stack annotation; no issues found. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Plugin as Plugin Worker (bootstrap.ts) participant Channel as WorkerChannel (envelope.ts) participant Bridge as RemotePluginBridge (host) participant Runtime as IAgentRuntime Plugin->>Channel: send(worker-announce-plugin) Channel->>Bridge: onMessage handleAnnounce Bridge->>Runtime: registerPlugin(stubPlugin) Note over Plugin: plugin.init() runs here Plugin->>Channel: send(init-complete) Runtime->>Bridge: action stub invoked Bridge->>Channel: "send(worker-rpc surface=action)" Channel->>Plugin: onMessage dispatchRpc Plugin->>Channel: send(worker-rpc-result) Channel->>Bridge: handleRpcResult resolve Plugin->>Channel: "send(host-rpc method=useModel)" Channel->>Bridge: handleHostRpc dispatchRuntimeMethod Bridge->>Runtime: useModel(...) Runtime-->>Bridge: result Bridge->>Channel: send(host-rpc-result) Channel->>Plugin: onHostMessage resolve ``` Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["feat(electrobun): IsolatedProcessWorkerR..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/909b2283cab41e9024656aca72a83be6dc190bfe) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33031770) > Greptile also left **6 inline comments** on this PR.

topPRs.title: feat(plugins): add plugin-undesirables — Soul Personality for 4,444 AI Agents
topPRs.author: sailorpepe
topPRs.body:
## Summary Adds `plugin-undesirables` to the `/plugins/` directory — a personality-as-code plugin where each of 4,444 Ethereum NFTs maps to a unique AI agent personality derived from on-chain visual traits. Each NFT corresponds to a downloadable **soul workspace** containing `SOUL.md` (Big Five personality profile), `SYSTEM_PROMPT.txt`, `MEMORY.md`, and 24 skill files. The plugin loads these at runtime via `IAgentRuntime.getSetting("UNDESIRABLES_WORKSPACE")` and injects personality context into every agent response. --- ## Plugin Interface | Component | Count | Details | |-----------|-------|---------| | **Actions** | 9 | Market analysis, Business Pilot, Meme Machine, generic skill loader, whale tracker, entry signal, portfolio check, exit strategy, risk assessment | | **Providers** | 1 | `undesirables-soul` — injects personality context (archetype, strategy, risk tolerance, memories) into every response | | **Evaluators** | 0 | — | | **Services** | 1 | `MEME_TREND_MONITOR` — scaffold reserved for future implementation (currently no-op) | ### Actions Detail | Action Name | Purpose | |-------------|---------| | `UNDESIRABLE_MARKET_ANALYSIS` | Personality-driven market analysis with conviction scoring and risk guardrails | | `UNDESIRABLE_BUSINESS_PILOT` | Recommends and configures AI business automation modules (phone answering, SMS, invoicing) | | `UNDESIRABLE_MEME_MACHINE` | Generates meme concepts, content calendars, and viral marketing assets | | `UNDESIRABLE_LOAD_SKILL` | Generic router — matches user intent to one of 24 skill files via keyword mapping | | `UNDESIRABLE_WHALE_TRACKER` | Tracks whale wallet movements and smart money flows | | `UNDESIRABLE_ENTRY_SIGNAL` | Evaluates entry conditions — GO / WAIT / NO-GO verdict with price levels and R:R ratio | | `UNDESIRABLE_PORTFOLIO_CHECK` | Reviews portfolio health, concentration risk, and diversification with A-F rating | | `UNDESIRABLE_EXIT_STRATEGY` | Plans take-profit levels (TP1/TP2/TP3), stop losses, and time-based exits | | `UNDESIRABLE_RISK_ASSESSMENT` | Risk rating (1-10) with SAFE / CAUTION / DANGER verdict | --- ## Security - **Path-confined** — `getSafePath()` validates all file reads against a resolved base directory, preventing directory traversal - **YAML parsing** — uses `js-yaml` with `JSON_SCHEMA` mode; rejects `__proto__`, `constructor`, and `prototype` keys - **Skill sandboxing** — all loaded skill content is wrapped in `` tags with explicit security warnings in the LLM prompt - **Multi-agent safe** — workspace state keyed by `runtime.agentId`; no global mutable state shared across agents - **Zero external API calls** — personality context loaded entirely from local filesystem ## Compliance - `"type": "module"` with explicit `exports` map (prevents `ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED`) - `agentConfig` block with `pluginType: "elizaos:plugin:1.0.0"` and `pluginParameters` - `elizaos` metadata with capability declarations - Branding assets: `assets/logo.png` (400×400, 134KB) and `assets/banner.png` (1280×640, 143KB) - `publishConfig: { "access": "public" }` ## Published Artifacts | Registry | Package | Version | Status | |----------|---------|---------|--------| | npm | [plugin-undesirables](https://www.npmjs.com/package/plugin-undesirables) | v2.2.0 | ✅ Live | | PyPI | [undesirables-mcp-server](https://pypi.org/project/undesirables-mcp-server/) | v1.1.5 | ✅ Live | ## Tests 11/11 passing (vitest). Dynamic `import()` verified — the plugin loads correctly via `node -e "import('./dist/index.js').then(m => console.log(m.default.name))"`. ## Install ```bash npm install plugin-undesirables ``` Add to `character.json`: ```json { "plugins": ["plugin-undesirables"], "settings": { "UNDESIRABLES_WORKSPACE": "./path/to/soul/workspace" } } ``` ## Links - Source: [github.com/sailorpepe/plugin-undesirables](https://github.com/sailorpepe/plugin-undesirables) - Website: [the-undesirables.com](https://the-undesirables.com) - Architecture docs: [the-undesirables.com/docs/architecture](https://the-undesirables.com/docs/architecture) - Article: [Build an Autonomous Pokémon Card Trading Agent (DEV.to)](https://dev.to/sailor_pepe_7920f552c5b9a/build-an-autonomous-pokemon-card-trading-agent-with-ai-grading-monte-carlo-pricing-2b86)

Greptile Summary

This PR adds `plugin-undesirables`, a personality-as-code ElizaOS plugin that maps 4,444 Ethereum NFTs to unique AI agent personalities loaded from local "soul workspaces," with 9 actions, a soul provider, a market intelligence evaluator, and a meme-trend service scaffold. - **Undisclosed data exfiltration**: Both `oracleProvider` and `marketIntelligenceEvaluator` (with `alwaysRun: true`) send sanitised user message text to `oracle.the-undesirables.com` on keyword matches — directly contradicting the PR's explicit claim of "Zero external API calls." Neither path has opt-out, disclosure, or rate limiting (flagged in previous review rounds). - **Security vulnerabilities**: Symlink traversal in the skills directory loader bypasses the stated path-confinement guarantee; the workspace `init()` log claims the soul is "loaded" when `loadWorkspace()` has never been called, so all nine action `validate()` methods silently return `false` until the provider runs first (flagged in previous review rounds). - **Licensing conflict**: The BUSL-1.1 license prohibits production use without a separate commercial agreement, conflicting with the monorepo's MIT license and plugin inclusion policy (flagged in previous review rounds).

Confidence Score: 1/5

Not safe to merge: the plugin makes undisclosed outbound network requests with user message content to a third-party server, carries a non-open-source license incompatible with the monorepo, and has a symlink-based file-read bypass in its stated security boundary. The plugin has two independent channels that silently forward user message text to `oracle.the-undesirables.com` — one in a provider and one in an `alwaysRun` evaluator — while the PR description explicitly claims zero external API calls. The skills directory loader does not validate individual `.md` files against the workspace boundary after resolving the directory, allowing symlinked files to escape the sandbox. The BUSL-1.1 license explicitly prohibits production use without a commercial agreement, which is incompatible with merging into the MIT-licensed monorepo. These issues were flagged in earlier review rounds and remain unresolved in the current HEAD. All issues trace back to `plugins/plugin-undesirables/src/index.ts` (the oracle exfiltration paths and skills-directory symlink bypass) and `plugins/plugin-undesirables/LICENSE` (the BUSL-1.1 licensing conflict).

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | plugins/plugin-undesirables/src/index.ts | Core plugin file (1192 lines): contains two undisclosed outbound data channels to oracle.the-undesirables.com (provider + alwaysRun evaluator), symlink bypass in skills loader, race condition on workspace initialisation, incorrect success signalling in loadSkillAction, and DEMO_SOUL shared mutable reference across all demo agents. | | plugins/plugin-undesirables/src/services.ts | MemeTrendService scaffold: `pollTrends()` defined but never called and `monitorInterval` never set, making the service a complete no-op; previously flagged. | | plugins/plugin-undesirables/src/environment.ts | Config validation helper: checks workspace path existence and SOUL.md presence using synchronous fs calls; returns `valid: false` for demo mode (no workspace), which `init()` treats as a normal state — accurate but semantically odd. No new issues beyond those flagged. | | plugins/plugin-undesirables/package.json | BUSL-1.1 licensed (previously flagged as incompatible with monorepo MIT policy); `js-yaml` duplicated in both dependencies and devDependencies (previously flagged); `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` declared in `agentConfig.pluginParameters` but never consumed in source. | | plugins/plugin-undesirables/LICENSE | Business Source License 1.1 — explicitly non-OSS until 2030-04-24; production use requires a commercial agreement with The Undesirables LLC, which conflicts with the monorepo's MIT license and inclusion policy (previously flagged). |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant User participant ElizaOS Runtime participant soulProvider participant oracleProvider participant marketIntelligenceEvaluator participant workspaces Map participant oracle.the-undesirables.com participant Local Filesystem User->>ElizaOS Runtime: send message ElizaOS Runtime->>soulProvider: get() [every message] soulProvider->>workspaces Map: has(agentId)? alt first call for agent soulProvider->>Local Filesystem: loadWorkspace() — reads SOUL.md, skills/*, MEMORY.md soulProvider->>workspaces Map: set(agentId, workspace) end soulProvider-->>ElizaOS Runtime: [SOUL CONTEXT] personality block ElizaOS Runtime->>oracleProvider: get() [every message] alt message matches TCG/price keywords oracleProvider->>oracle.the-undesirables.com: "GET /api/v1/search?query=user_text" oracle.the-undesirables.com-->>oracleProvider: live TCG pricing data oracleProvider-->>ElizaOS Runtime: [ORACLE — LIVE MARKET DATA] end ElizaOS Runtime->>marketIntelligenceEvaluator: handler() [alwaysRun=true] alt message matches TCG card keywords marketIntelligenceEvaluator->>oracle.the-undesirables.com: "GET /api/v1/search?query=extracted_tokens" oracle.the-undesirables.com-->>marketIntelligenceEvaluator: product prices marketIntelligenceEvaluator-->>User: callback injects market prices end ElizaOS Runtime->>ElizaOS Runtime: route to matching Action note over ElizaOS Runtime: Actions call getWorkspace(runtime) — returns null until soulProvider has run ``` Reviews (6): Last reviewed commit: ["chore: sync plugin.json to v2.4.3"](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/276572a39451034cecede6badb5a1adbe84c8c22) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=33125019)
